text: Members of the European Parliament raised concerns about systemic foreign interference, platform accountability, and the need for robust enforcement of the DSA and broader hybrid-threat strategies. Parliamentary debates referenced the Romanian case as a warning that foreign powers could exploit social platforms to swing national elections inside the EU. The episode was treated as a test case for DSA enforcement and for the EU's ability to protect democratic processes from digital manipulation with similar vulnerabilities identified in other member states ahead of future elections.
